fr.recommendations: Adjustments to the property to support emergency response efforts may include ensuring that access points are clearly marked and unobstructed. Maintaining open terrain around the main and secondary buildings would enhance the mobility of emergency personnel and facilitate efficient operations. Creating clearly defined pedestrian walkways and keeping these paths free of obstructions could ensure smooth navigation for responders. Strategically locating staging areas near the vehicle parking area would allow for quick deployment and resource allocation. Enhancing visibility throughout the landscaped and open green spaces may further streamline navigation and ensure swift access to key areas of the property during an emergency.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing wildfire resilience on the property could benefit from increasing the separation between the dense tree canopy and structures, which may reduce vegetation continuity and mitigate potential fire exposure. Preserving the open green spaces and ensuring that they remain clear of combustible material can improve the overall defensible space, acting as barriers to fire spread. Addressing vegetation adjacent to the structures by reducing or managing it may lower structure exposure and enhance the defensible space quality. The presence of scattered trees can be optimized by maintaining optimal spacing between them, potentially reducing fuel continuity. Given the multiple access routes, ensuring these pathways are clear and well-maintained could support firefighter access and operations. Initiating or continuing regular monitoring of the vegetated perimeter for encroachment will help maintain defensible space buffers and support long-term preparedness initiatives. Keeping open terrain free from increasing vegetation buildup can contribute significantly to reduction of contiguous fuel loads.
